resources.string: Adds haptic feedback to taps and toggles system-wide.

Hapticks listens for tap and switch-related accessibility events so it can trigger the device vibrator. It does not read, store, or transmit any on-screen content. You can turn it off at any time from Settings > Accessibility.
Pick the texture of each tap
Taps, switches, and toggles—uses the pattern and intensity below
Gentle tap like hitting a soft wall
Hapticks needs the accessibility service to fire haptic feedback when you tap or flip switches in any app. Your content is never read or stored.
Adds responsive touch feedback across apps and system UI for a more refined, premium feel
Feel a firm pulse the instant a scroll hits the absolute top or bottom in any app.
Adds a subtle, rhythmic click as you scroll for a more grounded, physical experience.
Feel a satisfying buzz when you plug in or unplug your charger.
Custom vibration feedback for volume, power, and brightness controls.
Turn on the Hapticks accessibility service under Settings → Accessibility. Edge pulses use the pattern and intensity you choose below.
Uses the accessibility scroll-edge path to fire a pulse when you hit the top or bottom of a scrollable surface.

Hapticks listens for accessibility scroll events and compares scroll position to the reported range. When you move onto the top or bottom edge from inside the content, it plays your chosen edge pattern once.\n\nLimits: Only vertical lists and pages that expose scrollY and maxScrollY through TYPE_VIEW_SCROLLED are supported. Horizontal carousels, many games, some WebViews, and heavily custom drawn surfaces often send no usable data, so you may feel nothing there. Pulses follow how often the app posts scroll updates, so timing can feel less tight than the LSPosed EdgeEffect path. The main Hapticks accessibility service must stay on.
Uses a more precise method to detect when you hit the edge of a scroll. Enabling this provides a cleaner feel and prevents double vibrations.

This switches the app to hook scroll edge effects via LSPosed (libxposed path), which tracks overscroll at the system level for a cleaner pulse and helps avoid duplicate vibrations with the accessibility scroll path. Use the steps below only when you intend to run the module in LSPosed.

For the best results, enable this app in LSPosed Manager: 

1. Open LSPosed.

2. Locate this app and enable its module.

3. Select all the apps you want to apply the module to in the module scope.

4. Restart the affected apps or your phone to apply changes.
Great! The module is running smoothly. Edge haptics will work in the apps you have selected.
To get started, turn on the Hapticks module in LSPosed Manager, set your app scope, and reboot or force-stop the selected apps. When you are done, come back to this screen.
LSPosed not connected, sad status icon
Use accessibility scroll events to fire a soft haptic cadence while lists and pages move
Also fire haptics when scrolling horizontally in carousels and pagers
How many pulses to play per about 100 pixels of list or page movement. Higher feels denser; lower feels more spaced out.
How strong each scroll pulse feels while you move through content.
Fixed number of vibrations fired per scroll event. Higher values add more texture to every movement.
Adds extra vibrations when scrolling faster. Higher values make fast flings feel more intense than slow drags.
Suppresses trailing vibrations that continue after you stop scrolling. Increase to silence more of the fling tail.
Play a haptic pattern when charger is connected or disconnected
Vibrate when charger is plugged in
Play a haptic when volume up or down is pressed
Play a haptic when the power button is pressed
Play a haptic tick when brightness is adjusted
Use a pure black theme, useful for AMOLED screens to reduce eye strain and save battery.
Applies when the app is in dark theme
